extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remko attacked solo 30km out on La Redoute, maintained 40-second gap through tactical hesitation behind, won by 48 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Bahrain Victorious burned matches through Landa/Caruso early attacks at 45km, softening field for Evenepoel's later move.
Chase group tactical paralysis on Roche-aux-Faucons allowed 18-second gap to balloon back over 30 seconds despite brief acceleration.
Evenepoel attacked seated on double-digit gradients La Redoute, suggesting power-based rather than explosive acceleration was the winning formula.
Quick-Step survived Alaphilippe crash/abandonment at 60km by pivoting immediately to Evenepoel as sole leader with no team support needed.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remko: positive: Won monument debut at 22 with 30km solo effort, showing grand tour-level sustained power reserves unprecedented for his age.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Crash at 60km forced abandonment, rivals were so concerned about him Wellens checked his condition before rejoining race.
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexander: neutral: Attacked at 8.5km during chase hesitation but couldn't bridge to Evenepoel, suggesting strong but not decisive climbing.
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: neutral: Led chase effort midway up Roche-aux-Faucons but gap only briefly reduced to 18 seconds before tactical games resumed.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Initiated Bahrain's early attacking strategy at 45km, role as pace-setter rather than winner despite being former LBL champion.
